title: Is Bliss, Then, Such Abyss
content:
Is bliss, then, such abyss
    I must not put my foot amiss
    For fear I spoil my shoe?

    I'd rather suit my foot
    Than save my boot,
    For yet to buy another pair
    Is possible
    At any fair.

    But bliss is sold just once;
    The patent lost
    None buy it any more.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Is Bliss, Then, Such Abyss", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
